hints.Surge only: There are two imperative verbs: 'Surge' (rise) and 'tolle' (take up)
hints.lectum and tuum: 'lectum' is a noun (bed) and 'tuum' is a possessive adjective (your)
hints.tolle only: 'tolle' is an imperative, but so is 'Surge' — both are verbs

hints.to surge forward: English 'surge' is related but 'surgere' in Latin means 'to rise/get up'

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'surgit' — 'Surge' is a command
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'surgat' — 'Surge' is the imperative singular
hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'surrexit' — 'Surge' is the imperative

hints.rego, regere, rexi, rectum: These are principal parts of 'rego' (to rule/direct)
hints.sumo, sumere, sumpsi, sumptum: These are principal parts of 'sumo' (to take up)
hints.curro, currere, cucurri, cursum: These are principal parts of 'curro' (to run)

hints.to endure: 'to endure' is 'tolerare' — 'tolle' comes from 'tollere' (to lift/take up)
hints.to destroy: While 'tollere' can mean 'to remove', its primary sense is 'to lift/take up'

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'tollit' — 'tolle' is imperative singular
hints.infinitive: Infinitive would be 'tollere' — 'tolle' is the imperative
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'tollat' — 'tolle' is the imperative

hints.tolero, tolerare, toleravi, toleratum: These are principal parts of 'tolero' (to endure)
hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are principal parts of 'teneo' (to hold)
hints.trado, tradere, tradidi, traditum: These are principal parts of 'trado' (to hand over)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'lectus' — 'lectum' with -um ending is accusative singular
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'lecto' — 'lectum' is accusative, direct object of 'tolle'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'lecti' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'lecti' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'lecti' ends in -i
